Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows markedly elevated creatinine and proteinuria, suggesting significant renal impairment, possibly nephrotic syndrome or advanced chronic kidney disease. Urine findings (cloudy, brown, low specific gravity, bilirubin, ketones, glucosuria) further support renal dysfunction. Electrolytes are mostly normal except for mild hyperchloremia. HbA1c is low, excluding chronic hyperglycemia. Immediate nephrology evaluation is recommended.
